title: Eleven Reckless Oscar Predictions. Plus: Bong Joon-ho! | The Oscars Show
format: Sean and Amanda open awards season with the Gotham nominees, a chaotic Little Women screening, and Robert Evans, then make early, hedged predictions in six Oscar categories. Sean closes with Bong Joon-ho, through translator Sharon Choi, on Parasite.

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: Gotham Best Feature nominee; the field's best long-shot for a Best Picture slot, carried by Lulu Wang's campaigning.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: Safdie Brothers, Gotham Best Feature. Sean thinks older voters get strafed by it and keep Adam Sandler out of Best Actor.

films.title: Waves
films.note: Trey Edward Shults, Gotham Best Feature. Sterling K. Brown wowed them but the movie may be too small to land nominations.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: Noah Baumbach; Amanda calls it a near-lock for Best Picture.

films.title: Hustlers
films.note: Its Gotham Best Feature slot means people take it as a capital-F film, though Jennifer Lopez went unnominated there.

films.title: The Last Black Man in San Francisco
films.note: Joe Talbot up for the Gotham breakthrough-director award.

films.title: Booksmart
films.note: Olivia Wilde in the same Gotham breakthrough-director field.

films.title: Burning Cane
films.note: Phillip Youmans, Gotham breakthrough director; unreleased and unseen by either host.

films.title: High Flying Bird
films.note: Andre Holland's Gotham acting nod, the kind of pick Sean says won't survive to the Oscars.

films.title: Diane
films.note: Mary Kay Place, Gotham Best Actress: a critics'-list performance.

films.title: Her Smell
films.note: Elisabeth Moss, Gotham Best Actress; Sean keeps meaning to see it.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: They saw it the week before; the closing pitch is to catch it in a theater before Netflix on Nov. 27.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: Loved it, but they don't expect it in the awards conversation.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: Greta Gerwig's version; a near-brawl over seats at the DGA screening read as real enthusiasm, and the hopeful ending is the kind the Oscars have rewarded lately.
Sam Mendes, shot by Roger Deakins; unseen, screening late (~Nov. 23), the wild card scrawled with 20 question marks on Amanda's sheet.

films.title: Richard Jewell
films.note: Clint Eastwood's Atlanta-bombing film, unseen; a possible late entry for Paul Walter Hauser.

films.title: Cats
films.note: Unseen. Sean lists it; Amanda is mad about it.

films.title: Dark Waters
films.note: Unseen, just starting to screen.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: Unseen; Sean read box-office prognostication tracking it as the lowest-opening episode so far.

films.title: Frozen II
films.note: Amanda's likely animated contender.

films.title: Clemency
films.note: Alfre Woodard, one of two death-row films this year; strong Sundance reviews.

films.title: Just Mercy
films.note: The other death-row film; Jamie Foxx in the supporting mix.

films.title: Joker
films.note: The biggest R-rated movie ever at the time; a Scott Feinberg voter survey had roughly half moved by it and few outright against it.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Tarantino; the revisionist happy ending fits the hopeful mood they see rewarded this year.

films.title: Jesus Is King
films.note: Kanye West's IMAX film; Sean, a longtime chronicler, still hadn't watched it (his read on how far Kanye has drifted from the culture's center).

segments.heading: Eleven Reckless Oscar Predictions
segments.note: six categories, two ballots, splits marked

categories.name: Best Supporting Actor
categories.note: split: Sean took Pacino, Amanda took Pitt

nominees.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
nominees.note: Amanda's winner; three prior nominations, never won, and a lot of movement behind him.

nominees.title: The Irishman
nominees.note: Sean's winner, picked that morning and already regretted: Pacino's the bigger of his two Irishman roles.

nominees.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood
nominees.note: Named a real threat in the category.

nominees.title: The Two Popes
nominees.note: Unseen by both; Amanda swayed by Lulu Wang praising it on Twitter.

nominees.title: Just Mercy
nominees.note: Sean's fifth pick.

nominees.title: The Irishman
nominees.note: Amanda's fifth pick over Foxx; a return to form that could split the Irishman vote with Pacino.

categories.name: Best Supporting Actress
categories.note: split: Sean took Dern, Amanda took Lopez

nominees.title: Marriage Story
nominees.note: Sean's winner in a highlight-reel year (also Little Women and Big Little Lies).

nominees.title: Hustlers
nominees.note: Amanda's winner, taken to keep it interesting; never nominated.

nominees.title: The Report
nominees.note: Sean's fifth pick; four nominations, no wins, and maybe on the cutting-room floor.

nominees.title: Bombshell
nominees.note: Both flag Bombshell as the likely vehicle, though neither had seen it.

nominees.title: The Farewell
nominees.note: Amanda's fifth over Bening: the grandmother, the heart of the film.

categories.name: Best Actor
categories.note: split: Sean took Phoenix, Amanda took Driver

nominees.title: Marriage Story
nominees.note: Amanda's winner; both call it the performance of the year, and he'll be top of mind with Star Wars out.

nominees.title: Joker
nominees.note: Sean's winner: the alien, Daniel Day-Lewis kind of performance other actors reward, if the film's problems don't hold him back.

nominees.title: Pain and Glory
nominees.note: Sean's pick; won Best Actor at Cannes.

nominees.title: The Two Popes
nominees.note: Sean's pick; somehow never Oscar-nominated.

nominees.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
nominees.note: Amanda's swap into the field.

nominees.title: Dolemite Is My Name
nominees.note: Amanda's last-minute swap over Banderas: glad-handing hard, hosting SNL, and clearly wants it.

categories.name: Best Actress
categories.note: both landed the same five; both take Zellweger

nominees.title: Judy
nominees.note: A months-long lock, boosted by a New York profile and a Telluride tribute.

nominees.title: Marriage Story
nominees.note: Very good, not great. The film ends up not really being about her.

nominees.title: Little Women
nominees.note: 25 and already three-time nominated.

categories.name: Best Director
categories.predicted: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood

nominees.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
nominees.note: The consensus winner: the wistful career-summation movie directors win for, à la del Toro and The Shape of Water.

nominees.title: The Irishman
nominees.note: A win they'd buy in a heartbeat: a guy still working with juice.

nominees.title: Marriage Story
nominees.note: Amanda's last-minute add over Sam Mendes; zero prior directing noms.

categories.name: Best Picture
categories.note: split: Sean took Once Upon a Time in Hollywood, Amanda took Marriage Story; ten from Sean, nine from Amanda

nominees.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
nominees.note: Sean's winner, off the Tarantino directing pick and preferential-ballot math.

nominees.title: Marriage Story
nominees.note: Amanda's winner: it's literally about a marriage, one of the most common experiences.

nominees.title: The Irishman
nominees.note: A lock for both.

nominees.title: Little Women
nominees.note: A lock for both.

nominees.title: Jojo Rabbit
nominees.note: A lock for both; treated as a red herring that could still pull nine nominations.

nominees.title: Parasite
nominees.note: A lock for Amanda; Sean argues it's not one: the year's hipster movie, closer to Phantom Thread than Roma.

nominees.title: The Two Popes
nominees.note: An actor-driven charmer with Telluride heat.

nominees.title: The Farewell
nominees.note: Sean's; Amanda leaves it off, betting it wins the screenplay instead.
Set aside as a lock only because neither had seen it.

nominees.title: Ford v Ferrari
nominees.note: Sean's; Amanda drops it, figuring it's forgotten if neither Damon nor Bale gets nominated.

nominees.title: Bombshell
nominees.note: Amanda's late add: this year's Vice, a performance-driven political film from the largest branch.

nominees.title: Joker
nominees.note: Amanda's realist add: huge money and a warmer voter sample than the early panic suggested.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Agreed to before Okja; he wanted Korean actors and a story grounded in Korea's reality. He calls it his staircase movie: the scholar's stone that gets announced as 'metaphorical' is the thing that ends up cracking a skull, because we never get crushed by metaphors. Only 10-15% shot on location; the flooded poor neighborhood was built in a water tank.

films.title: Okja
films.note: The one with the huge, lovely creature.

films.title: Snowpiercer
films.note: His 'hallway movie': a two-hour straight tunnel, the spatial constraint that lit a fire; shot by the same DP as Parasite.

films.title: The Servant
films.note: Joseph Losey; his Parasite double-feature pick.

films.title: The Housemaid
films.note: Kim Ki-young, restored by the Scorsese film foundation: a perennial in his top-ten lists, about a middle-class crisis and aged very well.

films.title: Lost Highway
films.note: David Lynch, cited as a slight inspiration for Parasite; Bong got to watch Lynch closely at the Governors Awards.

films.title: Blue Velvet
films.note: The other Lynch title he names as loosely inspirational.

films.title: First Cow
films.note: Kelly Reichardt, seen at Telluride: a quiet, poetic film he can't stop thinking about.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: His other 'last great thing': the loud opposite of First Cow, with a great Adam Sandler performance; he saw both plus Parasite in one Telluride day.

films: The Kid Stays in the Picture

The Last Black Man in San Francisco.title: The Last Black Man in San Francisco

Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood

A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood

The Kid Stays in the Picture.title: The Kid Stays in the Picture
